label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking for the main idea of the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand it. The passage starts by talking about London subways and how they're different from other cities. It mentions the historical value and that it's a must-visit. Then it discusses how people in London on the subway don't make eye contact, not out of rudeness but because they're too busy. The next part is about what they're busy doing—using technology instead of reading books. It goes into detail about apps like iShoot and Tube Exits, mentions Apple's profit from commuters, and talks about iPods and even palm DVD technology. The conclusion is that with all these distractions, people might forget to get off the train. So the main idea. Let's look at the options again: A) London commuters are unfriendly to strangers. The passage says avoiding eye contact isn't rudeness but that people are too busy. So A is probably not right because it's not about unfriendliness but busyness. B) Apple has earned a lot from 40k apps. The passage does mention Apple earning a fortune, but that's just one part of the text. The main focus isn't just about Apple's profit but about how commuters use technology in general. C) Technology is changing how London commuters spend their time. This seems to fit. The passage details various tech uses like iPhones, iPods, apps, etc., and contrasts them with old habits like reading books. The main point is about the shift due to technology. D) Riding a London subway is a must. The first paragraph says it's a must, but the rest of the passage is more about the behavior of commuters. So D
